svg.text: The work of being a stable, recognized institutional presence in the public health field. PHIs hold long-horizon relationships, take strategic
positions, and offer continuity that government public health (subject to political turnover) and short-cycle nonprofits often can't.
A durable, state-level mission lets the institute take
independent positions and go against prevailing political
winds, outlasting the cycles government and short-cycle
Retained staff accumulate institutional, historical, and
subject-matter knowledge, which feeds foresight and field
identity, which attracts funding and talent, which retains
Decades of long-held working relationships build funder
and organizational loyalty, legacy funders maintain and
renew, new funders become legacy. The 20-to-30-year
Funders churn constantly, but orgs need steady support.
The PHI absorbs the volatility through diverse streams,
fiscal sponsorship, and pass-through. Only as strong as
Political turnover shifts priorities and cuts funding
states can pursue. Because it's mission-rooted, the
institute applies on the state's behalf, keeping the plan
Running the buffer means chasing diverse and pass-through
funding, which creates pressure to be a fast,
transactional conduit. Pushed too far, that dilutes the
mission. Mission discipline is the lever.

text.tspan: Mission at the state level lets institutes go against prevailing political winds
The same knowledge engine Steward runs — the institutional-knowledge seam.
20-to-30-year relationships funders and orgs can't get elsewhere.
PHIs buffer funder churn into continuity. Only as strong as their own diversific
Keeper of the state health improvement plan even through political changes.

svg.text: Tension — mission discipline is the lever
